pattern: Because the array is a full permutation of 1..n, repeated indices let any single value and any pair-xor appear, so the reachable triplet xors fill an entire bit-range and the count collapses to a closed form on the bit length.
hints: Indices may repeat (i <= j <= k), so picking i = j cancels a pair and leaves any single element reachable as a triplet value.
With every value from 1 to n available, think about which bit positions can be set; the reachable values are exactly all integers below some power of two.
Handle n = 1 and n = 2 directly; for n >= 3 the answer is two raised to the bit length of n.

edge_inputs.description: value just above a power of two

stress.description: random permutation of 1..size, checked against the pairwise-set oracle
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = list(range(1, n + 1))
    rng.shuffle(nums)
    return [nums]
